data.change_summary: Seventh remediation round addressing 6 QA amendments: (1) Added clarifying doc comment at the OPENROUTER_API_KEY check site explaining BinaryNotFound is repurposed as a terminal 'infrastructure prerequisite missing' signal, and added a doc comment on the BinaryNotFound enum variant itself documenting its broader scope. (2) Restored exit code 127 → BinaryNotFound classification in classify_exit_failure: the function now matches on status.code() and maps Some(127) to BinaryNotFound (terminal), all other exits to TransportFailure (retryable). The _status parameter is now used. Updated the test to exit_code_127_returns_binary_not_found asserting BinaryNotFound. (3) Simplified backoff_for_attempt to compute the multiplier directly as 2u32.saturating_pow(attempt.saturating_sub(1)), eliminating the fragile u64→u32 conversion via try_into().unwrap_or(u32::MAX). (4) Fixed the service timeout +30s regression by adding an enforces_timeout() trait method to AgentExecutionPort (default false). ProcessBackendAdapter, TmuxAdapter, and OpenRouterBackendAdapter return true (they enforce their own inner timeout). The service now only adds the 30s buffer when enforces_timeout() is true; otherwise it uses request.timeout directly, restoring correct timeout behavior for stub and other lightweight adapters. (5) Exit 127 is now classified as BinaryNotFound (see item 2). (6) Fixed 3 engine availability rewrap sites (prompt-review refiner, final-review planner, final-review arbiter) to extract and preserve the original failure_class from BackendUnavailable errors instead of hardcoding failure_class: None, so BinaryNotFound from check_availability propagates correctly through panel dispatch.
outstanding_risks: Process group kill cannot be implemented due to #![forbid(unsafe_code)] — pre_exec(setsid) requires unsafe, and std::process::Command::process_group is nightly-only. Grandchildren spawned by claude/codex CLI may survive as orphans after the parent is killed on timeout/cancel.

The BinaryNotFound failure class name is used for both missing binaries and missing API keys (OPENROUTER_API_KEY). Doc comments and inline comments now clarify this dual use as a general 'fatal infrastructure prerequisite' signal, but operator logs will still show 'binary_not_found' for missing env vars. A future rename to FatalInfrastructure would be clearer but requires broader codebase changes.

The pre-existing flaky test service_emits_invocation_completed_trace_with_token_fields occasionally fails under high parallelism due to tracing subscriber interference between tests. This is unrelated to our changes and passes reliably in isolation.

steps.description: Added enforces_timeout() trait method to AgentExecutionPort with default false. Implemented enforces_timeout() returning true for ProcessBackendAdapter, OpenRouterBackendAdapter, and TmuxAdapter (delegates to inner process adapter). Added enforces_timeout() delegation to the BackendAdapter enum. Updated service.rs to conditionally add the 30s buffer only when enforces_timeout() is true, restoring correct timeout behavior for stub and other non-timeout-enforcing adapters.

Restored exit code 127 → BinaryNotFound classification in classify_exit_failure: function now matches status.code() with Some(127) → BinaryNotFound and _ → TransportFailure. The previously unused _status parameter is now actively used. Updated doc comment to explain the 127 convention. Renamed test to exit_code_127_returns_binary_not_found and updated assertion to match BinaryNotFound.

Fixed 3 engine availability rewrap sites to preserve failure_class from the original error: prompt-review refiner (engine.rs:5954), final-review planner (engine.rs:6430), and final-review arbiter (engine.rs:6439) now extract failure_class from the inner BackendUnavailable error via pattern matching instead of hardcoding failure_class: None. Non-BackendUnavailable errors still default to None.

Simplified backoff_for_attempt: replaced 2u64.saturating_pow(...) with try_into().unwrap_or(u32::MAX) chain with direct 2u32.saturating_pow(attempt.saturating_sub(1)), eliminating the lossy u64→u32 conversion entirely.

Added clarifying doc comment at the OPENROUTER_API_KEY check site in process_backend.rs explaining that BinaryNotFound is repurposed as a terminal 'infrastructure prerequisite missing' signal. Added doc comment on the BinaryNotFound enum variant in domain.rs documenting its broader scope beyond literal missing binaries.

Verified all 678 tests pass (cargo test), cargo clippy -- -D warnings clean, cargo fmt --check clean. Feature-gated verification: service_times_out_and_requests_backend_cancellation passes (timeout regression fixed), service_returns_backend_unavailable_as_transport_failure passes, exit_code_127_returns_binary_not_found passes.

validation_evidence: cargo test: 678 passed, 0 failed, 0 ignored across all test binaries
cargo clippy -- -D warnings: no warnings or errors

service_times_out_and_requests_backend_cancellation (--features test-stub): passes — stub adapter with 100ms delay and 10ms timeout correctly times out at the service level because enforces_timeout() returns false, so service_timeout equals request.timeout (10ms) without the 30s buffer
service_returns_backend_unavailable_as_transport_failure (--features test-stub): passes — stub BackendUnavailable with failure_class: None correctly defaults to TransportFailure (retryable)
exit_code_127_returns_binary_not_found: passes — exit code 127 from a running process now correctly maps to BinaryNotFound (terminal), preventing wasted retry attempts on missing binaries
spawn_missing_binary_returns_binary_not_found: passes — spawn() ErrorKind::NotFound path produces BinaryNotFound
All retry_policy_test tests pass: default limits, exponential backoff with 2u32 multiplier capped at 60s, no_backoff returns zero
All 72 workflow_engine_test tests pass with --features test-stub: retry exhaustion (5 attempts), resume-from-failed (6 stage entries), cancellation during backoff, and preflight failure tests
